subtheme_definition: Statistical methods, econometrics, regression, experimental design, causal inference, panel data, predictive modeling.
Ethnography, interviews, case studies, field methods, narrative/discourse analysis.
Courses explicitly integrating both quantitative and qualitative methodologies.
Psychometrics, assessment development, instrument design, reliability/validity testing, survey methodology.
Program evaluation, implementation analysis, impact evaluation, cost-effectiveness, benefit-cost assessment.

Use of coding, data mining, visualization tools, machine learning, or analytical software (e.g., R, Python, Power BI, Stata coding, dashboarding) to work with, analyze, and interpret education-related datasets.
Policy formulation, implementation processes, system reform strategies, change management.
Economics of education, funding models, resource allocation, ROI, cost structure of educational systems.
Testing policy, performance metrics, standards-based reforms, accountability systems.
Educator preparation, evaluation, teacher pipeline, professional development framed as policy.
Policy-making processes, federalism, institutional structures, political economy of education.
Policies addressing inequality, access, desegregation, cultural responsiveness, civil rights in education
Development of policy briefs, executive summaries, memos, and other nontechnical research translation formats.
Communicating with policymakers, practitioners, or communities; public-oriented messaging; partnership facilitation.
Using evidence to inform decisions, lead strategic conversations, influence policy direction.
Historical evolution of U.S. and international education systems, policy reform trajectories.
Theoretical perspectives on the purposes of schooling, ethics in education and decision-making.
Cultural contexts, inequality, identity, learning science, community effects, motivation, and development.
